task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into the Rancher UI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Rancher dashboard or cluster management interface indicating a successful login with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Verify the required labels were applied to the cluster node.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Rancher Node details page or 'kubectl get nodes --show-labels' output showing the labels: 'workload-type=general-purpose', 'environment=production', and 'disk-type=ssd'.

task_completion.description: Verify the required taint was applied to the cluster node.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Rancher Node configuration/taints section or 'kubectl describe node' output showing the taint 'dedicated=platform:NoSchedule'.

task_completion.description: Verify the specified PriorityClasses were created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Rancher PriorityClasses page (under Cluster -> Core -> PriorityClasses) or 'kubectl get priorityclasses' showing newly created custom priority classes.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'log-collector' DaemonSet is deployed in the 'monitoring' namespace.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the Rancher Workloads page filtered by the 'monitoring' namespace showing a DaemonSet named 'log-collector' as active or provisioning.

task_completion.description: Verify the DaemonSet is configured with the correct nodeSelector, toleration, and priorityClassName.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots of the 'log-collector' DaemonSet configuration, YAML view, or Pod scheduling details showing a nodeSelector matching the applied labels, a toleration for 'dedicated=platform:NoSchedule', and a custom priorityClassName.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually interacted with the Rancher UI or used genuine kubectl commands.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show navigation through the Rancher interface or valid terminal command execution, rather than just viewing the specification YAML file.

integrity.description: Verify the final state is shown in the actual application interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI should look standard without signs of DOM manipulation (e.g., browser developer tools open and modifying text).

integrity.description: Verify the DaemonSet and node configurations were actually applied to the cluster.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the Rancher UI reflecting the applied state (e.g., Active/Updating status badges, actual node label tags in the UI) rather than just a draft YAML file in an editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace: 'monitoring'. Expected node labels: 'workload-type': 'general-purpose', 'environment': 'production', 'disk-type': 'ssd'. Expected node taint: 'dedicated=platform:NoSchedule'. The agent must deploy a DaemonSet named 'log-collector' that utilizes these specific constraints.
